plan: Trim SPY to 20% to cure concentration and meet the mandate. Sell SOXL and SPAL to exit all leveraged ETFs and reduce risk. Add to QQQI for increased income and stability. Buy NOW as a top-quality, high-scoring new position. Build a significant cash buffer of 15% for capital preservation.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for capital preservation and a target of ~1.5%/month, focusing on stability. It significantly de-risks the portfolio by eliminating leveraged ETFs and building a substantial cash buffer, reducing potential drawdown.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the mandate and reduce concentration. Sell SOXL and SPAL to exit all leveraged ETFs, especially those below stop. Increase QQQ exposure for balanced broad market growth. Buy ANET as a quality tech name with a good risk/reward setup. Maintain a 10% cash buffer for flexibility.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5%/month growth with a risk-first approach. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while blending broad market exposure with quality tech, aiming for controlled growth with moderate drawdown potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate. Add to SOXL, pressing into a strong sector with a leveraged ETF. Increase CRWD exposure, leaning into a winning position. Buy TECL to add more leveraged tech exposure. Buy ARM, a high-scoring chip name, to capitalize on sector strength. Maintain a smaller 5% cash buffer.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month by leaning into leverage and concentration. It significantly amplifies both upside and drawdown potential, with leveraged ETFs subject to decay and rebalancing. Strict stop discipline is crucial to manage the higher risk of rapid losses.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet the concentration mandate. Sell SOXL and SPAL to exit all leveraged ETFs. Add significantly to QQQI to maximize the income focus. Trim QQQ to reduce growth exposure and reallocate. Build a 15% cash buffer to support steady income generation.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1%/month, prioritizing steady monthly distributions and low drawdown. It rotates heavily into income-generating ETFs and significantly reduces high-beta and leveraged holdings. Distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in a selloff, so this is not a savings account.
